methodsAttempted.query: site:dundee.ac.uk University of Dundee generative AI policy students assessment artificial intelligence guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official University of Dundee student GAI guidance, staff AI teaching and assessment guidance, a student acknowledgement guide, a LibGuides academic skills page, and the Quality and Academic Standards policies index.
Official Dundee pages were fetched with Firecrawl because direct plain HTTP from this worker received a Cloudflare challenge on www.dundee.ac.uk pages.
The official University of Dundee LibGuides academic skills page was checked as supplementary guidance and linked back to the central student GAI code of conduct.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Dundee sources provide public guidance on student generative AI use in assessments, acknowledgement and referencing, privacy and data-sharing risks, staff teaching and assessment practice, and AI detection tool limits. No central binding AI policy beyond these official guidance/code-of-conduct sources was found in this crawl.

artifacts.title: University of Dundee AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official University of Dundee guidance for students and staff on generative AI. The sources support narrow claims that Dundee does not impose a blanket GAI restriction in its student guidance, bars submitting AI-generated assessment answers as one's own work, treats unauthorised GAI use as academic misconduct, requires acknowledgement when GAI is permitted, warns against sharing personal or confidential material with GAI platforms, and advises staff not to use unauthorised AI detection tools. No central binding AI policy beyond these official guidance/code-of-conduct pages was found in this crawl.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Direct plain HTTP requests from this worker to www.dundee.ac.uk returned a Cloudflare challenge, so Firecrawl was used for official page extraction without bypassing access controls.
The Quality and Academic Standards policies index was rejected for claim extraction because it only links to the substantive GAI page and does not add policy-specific evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
